news.title: Prism Johnson's net profit grows 13.45% to ₹105.46 crore in FY26
news.para: Prism Johnson announced a strong financial year 2025-26. The company's net profit grew by over 13 percent. Total income also saw a healthy increase. The cement business performed well with higher sales volumes. The H&R Johnson and Prism RMC divisions also contributed to the overall growth. The company plans to raise funds through equity and debt.

news.title: Trichy: Construction & demolition waste recycling plant to come up in Panjapur
news.para: Trichy Corporation has approved a 4-acre site in Panjapur for a new construction and demolition waste recycling plant. This facility, a public-private partnership, will process 50 tonnes of debris daily, producing value-added materials like paver blocks and hollow bricks. The plant aims to curb illegal dumping and is expected to be operational by November 2026.

news.title: Noida: Anti-encroachment drive by GNIDA frees 40,000 sq metres of notified land in Bhanauta
news.para: Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority (GNIDA) successfully reclaimed 40,000 square metres of land, valued at Rs 80 crore, in Bhanauta village. The drive targeted illegal plotting and unauthorized constructions on land already acquired by the Authority. Officials are urging the public to verify land records before purchasing to prevent financial losses due to illegal colonies.

news.title: Jaipur: Experts seek relaxed setback norms in TOD corridors to boost development
news.para: Builders and urban planning experts are urging the Jaipur Development Authority (JDA) to relax setback norms for plots within proposed Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) corridors. They argue that rigid rules could reduce developable land and project viability, proposing a more flexible approach similar to other major Indian cities to ensure the policy's success.

news.title: Bagmane Prime Office REIT lists with 3.5% premium
news.para: Blackstone-backed Bagmane Prime Office REIT debuted on the stock market with a 3.5% premium, listing at Rs 103.50 on the NSE. The Rs 3,405-crore IPO saw strong investor participation, subscribed 23.71 times. The REIT owns six premium Grade A+ business parks in Bengaluru.

news.title: Lucknow development body to begin e-auction of 149 residential plots
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority is launching an e-auction for 149 residential plots in Gomti Nagar Extension and Basant Kunj Yojana, with prices ranging from Rs 8.5 lakh to Rs 1 crore. This is part of a larger sale of 475 properties valued at nearly Rs 3,000 crore across the city.

news.title: JSW Dulux posts net profit of ₹1,973.8 crore in FY26
news.para: JSW Dulux reported a significant jump in net profit to ₹1,973.8 crore for FY26, despite a 9.74% dip in total income. The company's Q4 FY26 saw a profit increase of 15.96% and a 6.2% revenue growth in its retained business, driven by strong volume growth in decorative paints. The board recommended a total dividend of ₹206 per share for FY26.

news.title: Ghaziabad: GDA board to discuss fire safety measures, land acquisition plans on April 14
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority's board will convene to discuss 17 proposals, including revised development fees and a Rs 100 crore allocation for fire safety in high-rise buildings. The meeting will also address land acquisition for Aerocity and the second phase of the Harnandipuram township project, alongside plans for an international cricket stadium.

news.title: Shivalik Small Bank closes in on ManiBhavnam Home Finance
news.para: Shivalik has received Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) approval for this acquisition while it has sought clearance from the Registrar of Companies. The deal comprises share swaps and a cash payment, people familiar with the matter said.

news.title: Haryana rolls out online mutation process to curb property fraud, begins with Manesar
news.para: Haryana has launched an auto-mode mutation system to combat property fraud, particularly preventing single plots from being sold multiple times. This online system automatically processes mutations upon property registration, eliminating the need for buyers to visit patwar offices and significantly reducing pending cases across the state.

news.title: Haryana: I-T department conducts searches tehsil office, detects ₹45,000 crore transaction discrepancies
news.para: The Income Tax Department conducted spot verifications at sub-registrar offices in Gurugram and Mohali, uncovering significant lapses in reporting high-value property transactions. Discrepancies worth Rs 45,000 crore were identified in Gurugram alone, with widespread inconsistencies in PAN details and buyer-seller information totaling Rs 65,000 crore nationwide.

news.title: Karnataka deputy CM launches ''Bhoo Guarantee'' scheme for 23 lakh Bengaluru properties
news.para: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister D K Shivakumar unveiled the 'Bhoo Guarantee' scheme. This initiative will digitize property records for 23 lakh owners in Bengaluru. A one-time settlement window will allow B-Khata properties to convert to A-Khata with reduced fees.  Concessions are also offered for planning violations and BDA properties.  Infrastructure projects like tunnel roads are also planned.

news.title: Delhi government considering lowering building height norm to strengthen fire safety measures
news.para: Delhi government is considering revising building height limits for fire NOC requirements following recent fatal accidents. Current guidelines exempt many residential buildings under 17.5 meters, leading to widespread bypass of fire safety measures. The move aims to mandate stricter fire safety for all residential structures, regardless of height.

news.title: LIC Housing Finance's net profit rises 2.97% to ₹5,604.24 crore in FY26
news.para: LIC Housing Finance reported a 2.97% rise in net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹5,604.24 crore. Total income grew 2.64% to ₹28,843.49 crore. The company also recommended a dividend of ₹10 per equity share and appointed Sandeep Kumar as COO.

news.title: Signature Global (India) posts net profit of ₹1,094.64 crore in FY26
news.para: Signature Global (India) has reported a significant surge in its net consolidated profit after tax, reaching ₹1,094.64 crore for FY26. The company also saw its total income grow to ₹2,778.85 crore. This strong performance was accompanied by a substantial reduction in net debt by 77%.

news.title: DLF's net profit rises 1.10% to ₹4,414.68 crore in FY26
news.para: DLF reported a modest 1.10% growth in net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹4,414.68 crore. Total income saw a 9.12% increase to ₹9,816.04 crore. Despite a dip in Q4 profit and income, the company recommended an ₹8 per equity share dividend and achieved ₹20,143 crore in new sales bookings.

news.title: UK's Vistry warns on profit, to slow house building in blow to shares
news.para: British house builder Vistry warned of lower annual profit and will slow construction due to the economic impact of the Iran War. The company expects first-half profit to be significantly below previous years, with an operational review underway. Vistry anticipates full-year adjusted profit before tax towards the middle of analyst forecasts.

news.title: Knowledge Realty Trust's net operating income rises 18% in FY26
news.para: Knowledge Realty Trust announced an 18 percent growth in net operating income to ₹4,048.4 crore. The board approved a distribution of ₹1.616 per unit for the quarter ending March 31, 2026. The trust reported a net asset value of ₹123.61 per unit. Net consolidated profit after tax for FY25-26 was ₹375.43 crore. Gross leasing reached 1.

news.title: Over 65,000 Chandigarh housing board homes under review for changes
news.para: Chandigarh administration has formed a committee to review 'need-based changes' in over 65,000 CHB houses. This move follows demolitions of unauthorized extensions, sparking protests. The committee, tasked with identifying regularizable modifications, will report within two weeks. Residents seek a one-time settlement, but only select changes are likely to be approved.

news.title: Haryana RERA initiates suo motu proceedings against Nitara Projects for alleged non-registration
news.para: Haryana RERA's Gurugram bench has initiated suo motu proceedings against Nitara Projects for allegedly developing, marketing and selling residential floors without mandatory registration under the RERA Act. The authority has issued a final opportunity to the promoter to respond, failing which ex-parte proceedings may be initiated.
